title: Interpret Chute Closure In Relation To Fullness And Exchange Timing
summary: Use the training source to interpret whether observed chute closure matches the documented behavior that chute closure occurs when the chute is full, and to compare that observation against the source discussion of exchange timing targets intended to complete exchanges before closure.
when_to_use: Use when reviewing or explaining an observed chute closure condition and determining whether it aligns with the source-described fullness-based closure behavior and exchange timing discussion.
not_for: Do not use as a corrective maintenance or control-adjustment procedure.
Do not use to invent thresholds, control logic, or recovery actions beyond what the source explicitly states.
Do not use when a root-cause diagnosis or corrective action is required but not supported by this source.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Observe chute status, compare the observation to the source explanation, and document whether the observed condition appears consistent with the source-described fullness and exchange timing behavior.

safety_notes: This is an interpretation/reference procedure only.
Do not invent control thresholds or corrective actions beyond the values explicitly stated in the source.
access_or_tools_needed: Source-described exchange timing and chute closure reference

access_required: Ability to observe chute status or review the relevant operational condition
Access to the referenced training source material
system_preconditions: A chute closure or exchange timing condition is available to observe or review
external_references: UPSFORTNA OptiSweep Training Day 1, segment training_video_day1_segment_0016

visual_references.description: Training frame describing chute-full behavior and exchange timing context, including the statement that chute closure occurs when the chute is full and that delayed exchanges increase exchange time.
Training frame describing tote exchange staging and chute timing behavior.
Training frame describing tote fullness thresholds and chute unavailable behavior when a tote is full.

steps.title: Observe chute closure and exchange pace
steps.purpose: Establish the observed condition that will be interpreted against the source explanation.
steps.instruction: Observe whether the chute is closed and note whether the operation appears to be keeping up with exchanges and tipping.
steps.expected_result: A clear observation is captured about chute status and whether exchanges and tipping appear to be keeping pace.
steps.healthy_condition: The observed condition is clear enough to compare against the source discussion of chute fullness and exchange timing.
steps.failure_condition: The observed condition is unclear or insufficient to compare to the source explanation.

actions.label: Observe chute status
actions.exact_action: Observe whether the chute is closed.

actions.label: Assess exchange and tipping pace
actions.exact_action: Note whether the operation appears to be keeping up with exchanges and tipping.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Look for the training discussion connecting chute closure with chute fullness and delayed exchanges.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the observed chute behavior cannot be clearly described well enough to compare with the source-described explanation.

source_evidence.text: Observe whether the chute is closed and note whether the operation appears to be keeping up with exchanges and tipping.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: It also notes chute closure occurs when the chute is full and that delayed exchanges can increase exchange time.

steps.title: Interpret closure as fullness-based behavior
steps.purpose: Apply the source explanation for why chute closure occurs.
steps.instruction: Use the source description to interpret the closure: the chute gets closed when the chute is full, not only based on whether both AGVs are in the station.
steps.expected_result: The observed closure is interpreted using the source-described fullness-based explanation.
steps.healthy_condition: The interpretation remains grounded in the source statement that chute closure is tied to fullness.
steps.failure_condition: The interpretation depends on unsupported assumptions such as closure being based only on AGV presence in station.

actions.label: Apply source explanation
actions.exact_action: Interpret the closure using the source statement that the chute gets closed when the chute is full, not only based on whether both AGVs are in the station.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Look for the statement that the chute gets closed when the chute is full.
Look for the tote fullness thresholds and the note that when a tote is full, the chute is unavailable.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the observed chute behavior cannot be reconciled with the source-described fullness-based closure explanation.

source_evidence.text: Use the source description to interpret the closure: the chute gets closed when the chute is full, not only based on whether both AGVs are in the station.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The chute gets closed when the chute is full.
When a tote is full, chat is told the chute is unavailable so no more packages are diverted there until an exchange is done.

steps.title: Compare observation to exchange timing targets
steps.purpose: Relate the observed condition to the source timing discussion intended to avoid chute closure.
steps.instruction: Compare the observed situation to the source timing discussion that exchanges are staged before 80% fullness, with a goal of median 8 second exchange time and a floor of 6.7 seconds.
steps.expected_result: The observation is compared against the source timing targets and staging discussion.
steps.healthy_condition: The comparison uses only the source-stated timing values and staging threshold discussion.
steps.failure_condition: The comparison introduces unsupported timing targets, thresholds, or control logic not present in the source.

actions.label: Compare to staging threshold
actions.exact_action: Compare the observed situation to the source discussion that exchanges are staged before 80% fullness.

actions.label: Compare to exchange timing
actions.exact_action: Compare the observed situation to the source timing values of a median 8 second exchange goal and a 6.7 second floor.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Look for the timing discussion stating exchange occurs before 80% fullness, with a median 8 second goal and 6.7 second floor.
Look for the exchange staging and chute timing behavior described in the training frame.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the observed timing or closure behavior cannot be compared to the source-stated values and staging discussion without making unsupported assumptions.

source_evidence.text: Compare the observed situation to the source timing discussion that exchanges are staged before 80% fullness, with a goal of median 8 second exchange time and a floor of 6.7 seconds.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Thresholds are staged so exchange occurs before 80%, with a goal of median 8 second exchange time and a floor of 6.7 seconds.

Thresholds trigger staging when a tote is imminently full or completely full... The transcript also states that the chute opens when the AGV moves in, not when it drops a tote, to meet quick KPI targets.

steps.title: Record whether the condition matches the source-described case
steps.purpose: Document whether the observed closure is consistent with the source explanation of delayed exchanges or slow tipping allowing the chute to fill and close.
steps.instruction: Record whether the observed closure appears consistent with the source-described case where delayed exchanges or slow tipping allow the chute to fill and close.
steps.expected_result: A documented interpretation states whether the observed closure is consistent with the source-described behavior.
steps.healthy_condition: The record clearly states whether the observation matches the source-described fullness and timing case.
steps.failure_condition: No clear determination is recorded, or the determination relies on unsupported corrective conclusions.

actions.label: Document consistency with source
actions.exact_action: Record whether the observed closure appears consistent with the source-described case where delayed exchanges or slow tipping allow the chute to fill and close.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use this training slide/frame as the image support for chute-full and exchange timing context.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the observed chute behavior cannot be reconciled with the source-described fullness-based closure explanation.
Stop and escalate if documenting the condition would require inventing control thresholds or corrective actions beyond the values explicitly stated in the source.

source_evidence.text: Record whether the observed closure appears consistent with the source-described case where delayed exchanges or slow tipping allow the chute to fill and close. [Image support needed: training slide or frame showing chute-full and exchange timing context.]

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: It also notes chute closure occurs when the chute is full and that delayed exchanges can increase exchange time.
If exchanges or tipping do not keep up, chutes eventually close because everything gets full.

success_criteria: The user can interpret chute closure as a fullness-related condition.
The user can relate the observed condition to the documented exchange timing targets discussed in the source.
The final interpretation remains limited to source-supported statements.
healthy_conditions: Chute closure is interpreted as a fullness-driven condition.
The observation can be compared to the source discussion that exchanges are staged before 80% fullness.
The interpretation uses the source timing values of median 8 seconds and 6.7 seconds without adding unsupported thresholds.
failure_conditions: The observed chute behavior cannot be reconciled with the source-described fullness-based closure explanation.
The interpretation requires unsupported assumptions about control logic or AGV station presence.
The user attempts to derive corrective actions or thresholds not explicitly stated in the source.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the observed chute behavior cannot be reconciled with the source-described fullness-based closure explanation.
Escalate if a root-cause diagnosis or corrective action is needed, because this source provides interpretation guidance rather than a full troubleshooting or corrective procedure.
Do not invent control thresholds or corrective actions beyond the values explicitly stated in the source.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Primary visual reference for chute-full and exchange timing context.
Supporting visual reference for exchange staging and chute timing behavior.
Supporting visual reference for fullness thresholds and chute unavailable behavior.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: The chute gets closed when the chute is full. Thresholds are staged so exchange occurs before 80%, with a goal of median 8 second exchange time and a floor of 6.7 seconds.

missing_details: The source does not provide a corrective procedure for resolving chute closure conditions.
The source does not define explicit operator actions beyond observing and interpreting the condition.
The source does not provide a formal escalation path, named owner, or downstream troubleshooting workflow.
The source does not provide commands, system navigation steps, or exact HMI page instructions for this interpretation task.
